title: photinia chingshuiensis
intro: Photinia chingshuiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the Taiwanese cherry or red tip photinia. It is a shrub or small tree native to Taiwan, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities. The plant's leaves, roots, and fruits are used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Photinia chingshuiensis are typically made into teas, infusions, or decoctions for internal use, or used topically as a poultice or salve for skin conditions and wounds.
